target: Cross-WIN sweep: replace the templated post-hoc data-source parenthetical "(IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records, etc.)" with per-WIN domain-appropriate source listings across 18 WINs. Curmudgeon WIN-060.c3 hole #2 (major) escalated by EXP-289 tracker entry; identical text appears in detail_verdict_text on WIN-002, 004, 005, 006, 007, 022, 024, 033, 041, 042, 043, 048, 052, 054, 059, 060, 063, 066. The wrapper sentence ("The confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset … that was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit") is unchanged on all 18; only the parenthetical contents change. ISS-1864 covered the broader "per-WIN appropriate text" sweep — this EXP closes the parenthetical-list piece of that ISS.
scope: 18 WINs, single substring replace per WIN, no other field edits

patch_instructions: For each entry in patches[]: in data/wins.json, locate the WIN matching win_id, find the literal substring old_substring inside the named field, and replace its FIRST occurrence with new_substring. Each WIN currently contains exactly one occurrence of the template (verified by analyst before write).

Do NOT modify any other field on any of these WINs. Do NOT modify the surrounding sentence in detail_verdict_text. The phrase "The confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset (...) that was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit" must remain otherwise byte-identical on all 18 WINs after this patch lands.

After applying patches, run tests/run.sh — there are no numeric or count claims in this edit, so all existing tests should still pass. Add a regression test if desired: assert that NO WIN.detail_verdict_text contains the literal substring "NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records" (the domain-mismatched list should never reappear).
holes_addressed: WIN-060.c3 hole #2 (major): templated post-hoc-test data-source list is irrelevant for this WIN domain. "Why does your post-hoc test for SAA drift mention ocean-tide gauges?" — exact dome-defender ridicule the curmudgeon flagged. Closed by narrowing each WIN to its actual data sources.

ISS-1864 (cleanup, parenthetical-list portion): per-WIN appropriate text in the post-hoc paragraph. The orphan-"the" piece of ISS-1864 was already fixed in commits 7678cc4 / 690ed3b; this EXP closes the data-source-list piece.
new_evidence_added: No new external citations introduced. Each replacement narrows to sources the WIN already cites in its own detail_evidence (e.g., WIN-006 already cites NOAA NP.xy, WIN-033 already cites Hipparcos, WIN-066 already cites Stephens et al. 2012 + CERES). The parenthetical now agrees with the body prose of each WIN instead of contradicting it.

anticipated_objections.objection: A uniform parenthetical was a feature, not a bug — it signals "we apply the same generic post-hoc test the same way everywhere." Per-WIN customisation could be read as cherry-picking which sources we mention.
anticipated_objections.response: The wrapper framing ("a field-specific published dataset … publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit") still establishes the uniform post-hoc test. The narrowed parenthetical only specifies WHICH field-specific dataset; the test itself does not change. Reading ocean-tide gauges into a Schumann-resonance WIN was inviting the reverse problem — a defender pointing out that we cited datasets unrelated to the claim. Specificity is more defensible than vagueness here.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon offered a cleaner option (b): just remove the parenthetical entirely and let the prose argue from each WIN's actual cited sources. Why pick option (a) and keep a list of sources at all?
anticipated_objections.response: Option (b) is genuinely defensible and slightly cleaner. We picked option (a) because (i) the EXP-289 tracker entry explicitly framed the work as "per-WIN appropriate text" rather than deletion, (ii) the parenthetical functionally documents which dataset was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit — keeping it makes the post-hoc argument self-contained without forcing the reader back into the body prose, and (iii) on cross-WIN consistency grounds, all 18 share the same paragraph structure and a sweeping deletion would create asymmetry with other WINs' post-hoc paragraphs that already specify a dataset. Option (b) is preserved as a follow-up if a future curmudgeon round prefers it.

anticipated_objections.objection: Some of these WINs are not really about the named single source — WIN-024 (Roaring 40s) is partly atmospheric and partly magnetic; WIN-066 (NH heat asymmetry) involves multiple datasets; WIN-054 (El Gordo) cites HST + Planck SZ together. A single parenthetical oversimplifies.
anticipated_objections.response: For mixed-domain WINs (024, 048, 054, 066) the replacement names two complementary sources joined by "plus" or a semicolon, not just one. The point is to remove sources that are clearly irrelevant (telluric records in a CMB-anomaly WIN), not to pretend each WIN has a single canonical dataset. Where two sources are genuinely both load-bearing, the parenthetical lists both.

anticipated_objections.objection: You picked the source names from the analyst's reading of detail_evidence, not from a structured code_analysis.data_sources_used field — which is empty/undefined on all 18 WINs. Could be wrong on a WIN where the body prose is itself sloppy.
anticipated_objections.response: Granted that an absent structured field is the right place to fix this longer-term — making code_analysis.data_sources_used a populated, audited field for every WIN would let future cleanups be programmatic rather than per-WIN-prose-reading. For this sweep, I used (a) inline-cited URLs in detail_evidence as the primary signal, (b) cross-checked against the curmudgeon WIN-060.c3 review's listed sources for the SAA group, and (c) defaulted to the most widely cited single source where multiple were available (e.g., NOAA NGDC NP.xy for the NMP-drift cluster). Where the choice was ambiguous I named two sources rather than one. A future EXP can populate code_analysis.data_sources_used as a structured field and let a regression test guard the parenthetical contents.

kernel_of_truth: The original templated parenthetical was a deliberate cross-WIN design choice — each WIN got the same boilerplate post-hoc test, applied uniformly, which is the right epistemics. The dome model legitimately is being checked against pre-existing public datasets, and stating that uniformly is a strength of our methodology. The kernel of truth in the original design: a uniform test reads as principled, not cherry-picked. The reason the kernel does not save the boilerplate as written is that the listed datasets are not in fact uniform across WINs — IGRF is irrelevant to WIN-033 (stellar photometry) and ocean-tide gauges are irrelevant to virtually all 18. The fix preserves the uniform-test framing (the wrapper sentence is byte-identical on all 18) while specifying which uniform dataset applies to which claim.
cross_win_consistency_note: The wrapper sentence "The confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset (...) that was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit, and we could not locate a pre-registration of the expected value in a timestamping venue independent of the author" remains identical on all 18 affected WINs after patching. Only the parenthetical contents differ. This preserves the cross-WIN structural symmetry while removing the domain-mismatch issue.
test_plan: After patch lands: grep across data/wins.json for the literal substring "NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records" — should return zero hits on all 18 patched WINs.
Run tests/run.sh — pass count should be unchanged (this is a prose edit, no numeric/count/classification change).
Optional regression test: assert that for each patched WIN, the wrapper phrase "a field-specific published dataset" still appears in detail_verdict_text (catches accidental over-deletion).
integration_notes: Decider should batch all 18 substring replaces in a single commit. Each replace targets exactly one substring occurrence per WIN (analyst pre-verified one occurrence per WIN). After the batch, ISS-1863 and ISS-1864 are fully resolved on the data-source-list dimension. Curmudgeon WIN-060.c3 hole #2 closed. Hole #1 (orphan-"the" regression) is already closed by prior commits 7678cc4 / 690ed3b — out of scope here.
